pattern: Counting each value's remaining frequency and looking up its complement turns pairing into a single hash-map pass instead of re-scanning the array for every element.
hints: For each value x, the only partner that can complete a pair is exactly k - x; you don't need to compare every pair of elements.
Count how many times each value occurs, then for each distinct value decide how many pairs it can form with its complement without double-counting a value against itself.

When x equals k - x, the value can only pair with itself, contributing count // 2 pairs; otherwise the pair count contributed is the smaller of the two values' remaining frequencies, counted once per complementary pair of values.

edge_inputs.description: a few clean pairs with leftovers
single element, can never form a pair
every value equals half of k, pairing with itself, odd count leaves one out
no two values sum to k at all
k smaller than the smallest possible pair sum, guaranteeing zero pairs

stress.description: large random array with a random target sum
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100000))
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 1000000000) for _ in range(n)]
    k = rng.randint(1, 1000000000)
    return [nums, k]
